metadata.discoveryMethod: Azure REST API policy assignment discovery plus effective-assignment reconciliation

excludedAssignments.reason: ArcBox-only assignment outside Contoso project scope

policies.name: sys.mfa-write
policies.display_name: Microsoft Azure Multi Factor Authentication Enforcement for Resource Write Actions
policies.description: Denies Azure control-plane write operations that are not performed from an MFA-authenticated user session.
impact_on_resources: All Azure resource create and update operations
policies.iac_implications: Bicep may validate successfully but deployments will fail unless the operator signs in with MFA or a tested automation identity path is allowed by tenant policy.

policies.name: AKS_LimitNodeCount_Deny
policies.display_name: Deny AKS deployment with agent pool count greater than 10
policies.description: Blocks AKS clusters that declare more than 10 agent pool profiles.
policies.iac_implications: Keep the Contoso AKS design at 10 pools or fewer. The current plan with dedicated system and user pools remains compliant.

policies.name: mcapsgov-vm-sku-deny
policies.display_name: MCAPSGov VM SKU deny set for H, M, and N families
policies.description: The MCAPSGov deny initiative blocks high-cost H-series, M-series, and N-series VM SKUs.
policies.iac_implications: Use supported general-purpose or memory-optimized SKUs such as Dv5 or Ev5 for the management VM. The planned D8s v5 stays compliant.
policies.required_value: Do not use H, M, or N-series VM SKUs

policies.name: block-classic-resources
policies.description: Blocks Classic compute, storage, and networking resource providers.
policies.iac_implications: Contoso already uses ARM resource providers only, so no plan change is needed.

policies.name: jv-enforce-rg-tags-v3
policies.description: The live assignment audits resource groups missing any of the tenant governance tags, even though the underlying definition is written as deny.
policies.iac_implications: Emit all tenant-required lowercase tags on the Contoso resource groups and preserve repo baseline tags separately where needed.
policies.actual_effect: Audit override on a deny definition
policies.azure_property_path: tags['environment'], tags['owner'], tags['costcenter'], tags['application'], tags['workload'], tags['sla'], tags['backup-policy'], tags['maint-window'], tags['technical-contact']

policies.name: jv-inherit-multiple-tags
policies.display_name: JV - Inherit Multiple Tags from Resource Group
policies.description: Auto-remediates missing child-resource tags by copying them from the resource group. The assignment currently uses `tech-contact` as the ninth key.
impact_on_resources: All child resources under the project resource groups
policies.iac_implications: Set both `technical-contact` and `tech-contact` on the resource group and continue setting explicit tags in modules where consistent reporting matters.

policies.name: storage-disable-local-auth
policies.display_name: SFI-ID4.2.1 Storage Accounts - Safe Secrets Standard
policies.description: Auto-remediates storage accounts so shared-key authentication is disabled unless the exclusion tag `SecurityControl=Ignore` is present.
policies.iac_implications: Set `allowSharedKeyAccess: false` directly in Bicep for the shared storage account used by Blob and Azure Files. Do not rely on post-deployment remediation.

policies.name: storage-no-blob-anonymous-access
policies.display_name: Ensure secure access to storage account containers
policies.description: Auto-remediates non-FileStorage accounts so anonymous blob access is disabled.
policies.iac_implications: Set `allowBlobPublicAccess: false` in the storage account module. This is mandatory for the Blob workload and harmless for the Azure Files use case.

policies.name: vm-managed-identity-remediation
policies.display_name: Add system-assigned managed identity to enable Guest Configuration assignments on virtual machines with no identities
policies.description: Auto-remediates qualifying Windows client VMs that have no managed identity by adding a system-assigned identity.
policies.iac_implications: Configure the management VM with managed identity explicitly. If the VM uses a Linux image or already declares identity, this policy will not need to intervene.

policies.name: azure-security-baseline
policies.description: Tenant-wide audit initiative covering NSGs on subnets, storage private link, Azure SQL private endpoints, APIM virtual network usage, TLS baselines, and other service hardening checks.
policies.iac_implications: Implement the Contoso private-network design directly in Bicep because the initiative audits many of the controls but does not block them. Keep NSGs, private endpoints, APIM network integration, and TLS settings explicit.

policies.name: gdpr-2016-679
policies.description: Subscription-level regulatory compliance initiative used for GDPR evidence and posture tracking.
impact_on_resources: All Azure resources in the subscription
policies.iac_implications: There is no discovered live region deny, so the implementation plan must enforce EU-only regions and GDPR-aligned private data paths directly in IaC.

policies.name: pci-dss-v4
policies.description: Subscription-level regulatory compliance initiative used for PCI evidence and audit tracking.
impact_on_resources: All Azure resources in the subscription
policies.iac_implications: Keep network segmentation, TLS 1.2, WAF, and private service connectivity explicit in Bicep even though the policy assignment audits rather than denies.

policies.name: asc-data-protection
policies.description: Defender for Cloud data-protection assignment that monitors secure configuration across data services in the subscription.
impact_on_resources: Data services covered by Defender for Cloud
policies.iac_implications: Treat this as a validation layer, not as a deployment blocker. Keep storage and data-service security properties explicit in the implementation plan.

policies.name: asc-opensource-relational-databases-protection
policies.description: Defender for Cloud assignment for open-source relational databases, relevant to PostgreSQL Flexible Server governance and security posture.
policies.iac_implications: PostgreSQL is not blocked by a live deny in this tenant, so the plan must still enforce Azure AD auth, TLS 1.2, HA, private access, and diagnostics directly rather than relying on policy.
